results.rendered_html: nikaṭa (1. ni + kaṭa) adj. zur Seite befindlich, nahe gelegen; subst. (m. n.  SIDDH. K. 249,a,3 . 4 ) Nähe  AK. 3,2,16 . 3,4,32,15 . ( COLEBR. 3,4,28,15 ).  H. 1450 . gamyatāṃ kiṃcinnikaṭaṃ saraḥ  PAÑCAT. 77,15 . nikaṭībhūta der sich genähert hat  KATHĀS. 19,87 . ānītāṃ rājanikaṭam in die Nähe von, zu 3,73 . sa ca prāpa nikaṭaṃ bhogavarmaṇaḥ 5,68 . 10,96 . 111 . 157 .  VID. 81 .  RĀJA-TAR. 6,14 .  Z. d. d. m. G. 14,573,9 . nikaṭāt  SOM. NAL. 105 .  RĀJA-TAR. 2,165 . nikaṭe  P. 4,4,73 .  ŚĀNTIŚ. 3,2 .  KATHĀS. 3,75 . 6,135 .  BHĀG. P. 8,8,24 .  PAÑCAT. 59,7 . nikaṭavartin 140,25 .⫽print_change

results.rendered_html:
nikaṭa a. Near, close, hard by, proximate.
— ṭaḥ, -ṭaṃ Proximity. ( nikaṭe is used adverbially in the sense of ‘near’, ‘at hand’, ‘hard or close
(pb) by;’ vahati nikaṭe kālasrotaḥ samastabhayāva haṃ  Śānti. 3. 2. ).
